title: Making French Fries Healthier with Microwaves
summary: French fries are tasty but can be unhealthy because they soak up a lot of oil when fried. Scientists at the University of Illinois are trying to make fries healthier by using microwaves during frying. They found that microwaves can help reduce the oil that gets into the fries while keeping them crispy. This method might also make cooking faster. The researchers used a special microwave fryer and tested potatoes to see how much oil they absorbed. They explained that when food cooks, water inside turns to steam and pushes oil out, but sometimes oil gets sucked in. Microwaves help by creating more steam inside the fries, which keeps the oil out. This could help people enjoy fries with less fat and fewer calories.

keywords.explanation: a type of energy that heats food from the inside out
a liquid used for frying that can make food unhealthy if too much is absorbed
a texture that is crunchy and firm, like good french fries
water vapor that forms when water gets hot and helps push oil out of food
a part of food that gives energy but can be unhealthy in large amounts

questions.question: What are scientists trying to make healthier?
How do microwaves help reduce oil in fries?
What university are the researchers from?
What texture do the scientists want to keep in the fries?
What does steam do to help keep oil out?
Why might this new method be good for large-scale food production?

background_read: French fries are a popular snack made by frying potato strips in oil, but they often have a lot of fat, which can lead to health problems like obesity. Scientists are exploring new ways to cook them to reduce oil absorption while keeping the taste and texture people love. This involves using microwaves, which heat food differently than traditional methods, to create steam that prevents oil from entering the fries. Understanding this can help kids learn about healthy eating and food science.
Article_Structure: WHO: Researchers at the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign
WHAT: Studying how microwave frying can make french fries healthier by reducing oil absorption
WHERE: In a laboratory setting, possibly at the university or with partners like Washington State University
WHY: To help people enjoy fried foods with less fat and fewer health risks

perspectives.description: This research aims to improve public health by reducing fat in popular foods, making it easier for people to make healthier choices without sacrificing taste.
